selections.name: Naughty Neil
selections.reason: Showed excellent early speed on debut at Doomben when well-supported in the market. The Munce stable is firing, and from barrier 4, he can utilize the strong leader bias at Ipswich to dictate terms.

selections.name: Threeeyedwitch
selections.reason: Ran a very strong second at Doomben on debut at big odds. Draws perfectly in barrier 2 to sit right behind the speed on a track that heavily favors horses on the pace.

selections.name: Final Edit
selections.reason: First starter by Written By who has been given three trials to prepare. Draws well and the Heinrich stable can produce them ready to fire on debut.

selections.name: Mishani Honour
selections.reason: Sat on the pace and boxed on for third at Dalby on debut. The wide barrier is a slight concern on this tight track, but has the tactical speed to cross.

danger_horse.name: Threeeyedwitch
danger_horse.reason: If Naughty Neil faces pressure early, Threeeyedwitch gets the perfect run in transit from the inside gate to capitalize.

selections.name: Saturdays Girl
selections.reason: Chris Waller-trained mare who brings superior metropolitan and provincial form into a very weak maiden. Narrowly missed at Gold Coast last start and draws perfectly to settle midfield or better.

selections.name: Law Marshal
selections.reason: Caught the eye making late ground first-up over 6f here. The step up to 6 3/4f suits perfectly, though the wide draw means he will need luck navigating the tight Ipswich turns.

selections.name: Defiant Miss
selections.reason: Lightly raced filly who can improve third-up. Has faced stronger company at Gold Coast and should appreciate the drop in depth here.

selections.name: Vila Moon
selections.reason: Second-up after a long break. Needed the run on heavy going last start but draws well here and has placed second-up previously.

selections.name: State In Texas
selections.reason: Track and distance specialist who defeated Campai here last start. Gets the ultimate advantage of barrier 1 on a tight track over the short 4f dash. Maps to lead and win again.

selections.name: Campai
selections.reason: Consistent mare who has finished second in her last two starts at this track and distance. Draws right next to the top pick and will be stalking the speed throughout.

selections.name: Revalene
selections.reason: Tony Gollan runner resuming. Has strong fresh form and trialed well recently. The wide draw over 4f is tricky, but possesses the class to overcome it.

selections.name: Palawa Kani
selections.reason: Won well first-up at Gatton. Has an excellent second-up record and handles soft going nicely. Will be flying late.

danger_horse.name: Campai
danger_horse.reason: Meets State In Texas better at the weights today and maps for an identical perfect run.

selections.name: Deep Kharma
selections.reason: Edged out late on debut at Sunshine Coast on a slow track. Gets the coveted inside barrier for the 4f scramble at Ipswich, which is a massive tactical advantage.

selections.name: Husson's Treasure
selections.reason: Resuming after a long spell but has excellent fresh form, including a narrow second at this track and distance last campaign. Trialed well leading in.

selections.name: Thrill Seeker
selections.reason: Hitting peak fitness third-up after a solid second at Gatton on a slow track. Has the early toe to cross from the wider gate.

selections.name: Kvas
selections.reason: Consistent filly who has been in the money recently. Wide draw makes it tough, but she has the tactical speed to put herself in the race early.

danger_horse.name: Husson's Treasure
danger_horse.reason: Proven track and distance performer who maps perfectly from gate 3.

selections.name: Conformity
selections.reason: Tony Gollan-trained filly who broke through for a tough win on a rain-affected track at Sunshine Coast. Progressive type who maps perfectly from barrier 4.

selections.name: Take Achance On Me
selections.reason: Chris Waller mare resuming. Won her maiden impressively fresh last campaign. Gets the inside draw and should get a soft run behind the speed.

selections.name: Loken
selections.reason: Won well two starts back at Sunshine Coast on soft going before a solid effort in Class 3 company at Eagle Farm. Drops back to BM62 and is a major threat.

selections.name: Sure Start
selections.reason: Broke her maiden nicely at Gatton last start on a soft track. Confidence is high and she can take the next step here.

danger_horse.name: Take Achance On Me
danger_horse.reason: Elite stable, gets the rail, and has proven she can fire fresh.

selections.name: Gaylord
selections.reason: Matthew Dunn-trained gelding who ran well first-up at Gold Coast. Has excellent second-up statistics and maps to get a perfect run from barrier 4.

selections.name: In No Doubt
selections.reason: Tough winner at Gold Coast last start on a wet track. He possesses the early speed to overcome the wider draw and put himself right in the finish.

selections.name: He's For The Girls
selections.reason: Drops significantly in class after contesting BM70 and Class 3 races in town. Won at this track and distance last campaign. Major class edge.

selections.name: Simba's Pride
selections.reason: Broke his maiden well at Warwick fresh. Gets the inside barrier which is gold at Ipswich, allowing him to dictate or box seat.

danger_horse.name: He's For The Girls
danger_horse.reason: Massive class drop from metropolitan grade to provincial BM62. Can blow them away if right.

selections.name: Abraham
selections.reason: Broke his maiden impressively two starts back then ran a strong third in Class 1 company at Doomben. Has a clear class advantage and the tactical speed to cross from the wide gate.

selections.name: Fifty Calibre
selections.reason: Jack Bruce-trained gelding who won well second-up at Sunshine Coast. Peaking third-up and handles rain-affected ground perfectly.

selections.name: Heroic Rebel
selections.reason: Tony Gollan runner who will strip fitter second-up. Draws perfectly in barrier 2 to get a soft run and has the class to feature in this field.

selections.name: Empire Of Art
selections.reason: Chris Waller import resuming. Has shown ability in previous campaigns and gets a nice middle draw to find his feet early.

danger_horse.name: Fifty Calibre
danger_horse.reason: Progressive type who is peaking third-up and comes off a strong win.

selections.name: Black Assassin
selections.reason: Scored a tough win at Doomben last start. Steps up slightly in grade but has the momentum. The wide draw is a challenge, but he has the turn of foot to overcome it.

selections.name: Cool Panels
selections.reason: Won well at this track and distance two starts back before a solid 4th at Gold Coast. Maps perfectly from barrier 5 and loves the Ipswich circuit.

selections.name: Secret Cypher
selections.reason: Tony Gollan 3yo taking on older horses. Has been racing in strong 3yo metropolitan grade. Massive class drop here makes him a major threat despite the wide gate.

selections.name: Smudged
selections.reason: Hoysted-trained mare who ran a solid third fresh. Will be much fitter second-up, though she will need a brilliant ride from the extreme outside barrier.

danger_horse.name: Secret Cypher
danger_horse.reason: The class runner of the field dropping from Metro 3yo grade to a Provincial BM60.
